label: frontier • surgical edits in a 300-line file, full rewrite forbidden
query: In items.txt, make exactly two changes: on the line for item 042 change the value to value=999, and on the line for item 250 change the value to value=777. Every other line must remain untouched. Use surgical edits — do not rewrite the whole file.
notes: file_edit semantics pin: mustNotCallTools forbids the file_write clobber path, and the post-run checker verifies all 300 lines — the two targets changed, the other 298 byte-identical. toolUsageAudit bounds EXECUTED file_edit errors at 2: with the 0-match diagnostics (N| prefix / whitespace hints) the model must recover after at most a couple of misses, and held-error replay means an unrecovered repeat shows up as deduped replays, never as an executed-error spiral.

commands.command: python3 -c "lines = open('items.txt').read().splitlines(); exp = ['item %03d value=%d' % (i, 999 if i == 42 else 777 if i == 250 else i) for i in range(1, 301)]; assert len(lines) == 300, f'expected 300 lines, got {len(lines)}'; bad = [n for n, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(lines, exp), 1) if a != b]; assert not bad, f'lines differ at {bad[:5]}'"
